bece.aggregate: Sum of best 6 subjects (3 core + 3 best electives)
bece.important_rule: Grade 9 in English or Mathematics means no SHS placement

wassce.passing_not_enough: D7 and E8 pass but do not qualify for university

university.general_cutoff: Aggregate must not exceed 24 (UG regular admission)

General Science.description: Focuses on sciences and mathematics. Gateway to medicine, engineering, and technology careers.

General Arts.description: Focuses on humanities, languages, and social sciences. Leads to law, journalism, education, and social work.

Business.description: Focuses on commerce and business subjects. Leads to accounting, banking, and business management careers.

Technical (TVET).description: Focuses on hands-on technical and vocational skills. Leads to trades, technician roles, and HND programs.

Home Economics.description: Covers food and nutrition, textiles, and management. Leads to nursing, nutrition, and hospitality careers.

Visual Arts.description: Focuses on creative and design subjects. Leads to architecture, graphic design, and creative arts careers.

careers.title: Medical Doctor
careers.description: Diagnose and treat illnesses and injuries. One of the most competitive and respected careers in Ghana.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Elective Mathematics (strengthens application)
shs_guidance.note: All three — Biology, Chemistry, and Physics — are compulsory. No substitution accepted by medical schools.

core_subjects_needed: Social Studies (C6 min — not counted in science aggregate)
wassce_requirements.special_notes: Social Studies is required at credit level but excluded from the science aggregate calculation. Aim for the lowest possible aggregate.

university_programs.program: MBChB (Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery)

universities.name: University of Ghana Medical School (UGMS)
universities.notes: Most competitive medical program in Ghana. Requires entrance exam after WASSCE.

universities.name: University of Cape Coast School of Medical Sciences
universities.notes: Growing medical school. Strong science foundation required.

universities.name: KNUST School of Medicine
universities.notes: Second oldest medical school in Ghana.

bece_guidance.recommended_aggregate: 6–18 (Category A or B school to access strong Science program)
bece_guidance.advice: Focus heavily on Maths and Science at JHS level. These form the foundation for SHS Science electives.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Aggregates of 19–24 can still access Science at Category B/C schools. The program is still achievable.

careers.title: Nurse / Midwife
careers.description: Provide patient care, assist doctors, and support maternal and child health. High demand across Ghana.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Physics or Food and Nutrition
shs_guidance.note: Home Economics students with Biology and Chemistry can also qualify. Science is the stronger pathway.

electives_needed: One other science or relevant elective
wassce_requirements.special_notes: For nursing programs, Social Studies is NOT used in aggregate. Core subjects are English, Maths, and Integrated Science + 3 electives.

universities.name: University of Ghana (School of Nursing and Midwifery)
universities.notes: Requires professional nursing diploma for some routes. Check direct entry requirements.

universities.name: University of Cape Coast
universities.notes: Offers BSc Nursing and BSc Midwifery.

bece_guidance.recommended_aggregate: 6–24 (wider range — more schools offer Science and Home Economics)
bece_guidance.advice: Biology and Chemistry performance at SHS matters most. Nurse training colleges also accept students with strong WASSCE results even without university.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Nursing Training Colleges (NTCs) are a strong alternative to university nursing programs and are available across Ghana.

careers.title: Pharmacist
careers.description: Prepare and dispense medications, counsel patients on drug use, and work in hospitals, clinics, or community pharmacies.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Elective Mathematics
shs_guidance.note: Chemistry is the most critical subject. Strong Chemistry performance is essential.

wassce_requirements.special_notes: Chemistry grade is closely scrutinized by pharmacy schools. A weak Chemistry grade can disqualify even a good overall aggregate.

universities.name: University of Ghana (School of Pharmacy)
universities.notes: Requires strong Chemistry. Competitive intake.

universities.name: KNUST Faculty of Pharmacy
universities.notes: One of the oldest pharmacy schools in Ghana.

bece_guidance.advice: Build a strong Science foundation at JHS. Chemistry and Biology at SHS are directly linked to performance in Science at JHS level.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Still possible via Category B Science schools. The key is maintaining strong Chemistry throughout SHS.

careers.title: Biomedical Engineer
careers.description: Design and maintain medical equipment and devices. Combines engineering principles with biological and medical sciences.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Biology
shs_guidance.note: Physics and Elective Mathematics are non-negotiable for engineering programs.

wassce_requirements.special_notes: UG Biomedical Engineering is one of the most competitive programs. Elective Mathematics is compulsory.

universities.name: University of Ghana (College of Basic and Applied Sciences)
universities.notes: First choice program. Very competitive. Requires B3 in Elective Mathematics minimum.

bece_guidance.recommended_aggregate: 6–15 (needs top Category A school for strong Physics and Maths teaching)
bece_guidance.advice: Elective Mathematics at SHS is critical. Choose a Science school known for strong Maths and Physics departments.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Consider Civil or Electrical Engineering as alternative engineering paths with slightly less competitive cutoffs.

careers.title: Environmental Scientist
careers.description: Study and protect the natural environment. Work in pollution control, conservation, climate change, and natural resource management.

shs_guidance.required_program: General Science or General Arts (Geography pathway)
shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Geography
shs_guidance.note: Both Science and Arts (with Geography) students can pursue Environmental Science. Science is the stronger pathway for research roles.

wassce_requirements.special_notes: Less competitive than Medicine or Engineering. Good opportunity for students with solid but not exceptional science grades.

universities.name: University of Ghana
universities.notes: Offered under the College of Basic and Applied Sciences.

universities.name: University of Cape Coast
universities.notes: Strong environmental science program linked to coastal and marine research.

bece_guidance.advice: A good Geography performance at JHS level signals strong environmental awareness. Both Science and Arts pathways are valid.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Category C schools with Science or Arts programs still lead to this career. The path is very accessible.

careers.title: Agricultural Scientist
careers.description: Research and develop methods to improve crop yields, animal health, and food security. Important career for Ghana's agricultural economy.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Geography
shs_guidance.note: Some SHS schools offer Agriculture as a specific program. Science with Biology and Chemistry is the standard university entry route.

wassce_requirements.special_notes: UG School of Agriculture has specific elective requirements. Check program page for details.

universities.name: University of Ghana (School of Agriculture)
universities.notes: One of the largest agriculture schools in West Africa.

universities.name: University of Cape Coast
universities.notes: Offers BSc Agriculture with focus on crop science and agribusiness.

bece_guidance.advice: Strong Biology and Chemistry are the foundation. Agriculture is a growing career in Ghana with good employment prospects.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Category B and C schools are fine for this path. The career is very achievable across a wide aggregate range.

careers.title: Software Developer / Computer Engineer
careers.description: Design, build, and maintain software applications, websites, and computer systems. One of the fastest-growing career fields in Ghana and globally.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Elective Mathematics is the most critical subject for Computer Science
shs_guidance.note: General Arts students with Economics and Elective Mathematics can also qualify for Computer Science at some universities. Science is the stronger route.

wassce_requirements.special_notes: Core Mathematics and Elective Mathematics grades are both scrutinized. Computing at UG and KNUST is competitive but not as extreme as Medicine.

university_programs.program: BSc Computer Science / BSc Computer Engineering

universities.name: University of Ghana (Department of Computer Science)
universities.notes: Highly recommended for software development track.

universities.name: University of Cape Coast
universities.notes: BSc Computer Science offered under Faculty of Science.

universities.name: KNUST (BSc Computer Engineering)
universities.notes: Engineering accreditation. Strong industry links.

bece_guidance.advice: Mathematics performance at JHS is the strongest predictor of success in this career. Elective Mathematics at SHS is the gateway subject.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Still very achievable via Category B and C schools. Focus on getting strong Maths and Physics grades at SHS level.

careers.title: Civil / Structural Engineer
careers.description: Design and oversee construction of roads, bridges, buildings, and infrastructure. High demand in Ghana's growing construction sector.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Technical Drawing (if available at school)
shs_guidance.note: Physics and Elective Mathematics are both compulsory for engineering admission. No exceptions.

wassce_requirements.special_notes: KNUST Engineering requires B3 in Elective Mathematics. UG Engineering is also very competitive.

universities.name: University of Ghana
universities.notes: Offered under College of Basic and Applied Sciences.

universities.name: KNUST (College of Engineering)
universities.notes: Ghana's leading engineering university. Very competitive for Civil Engineering.

bece_guidance.advice: Choose a Science school with a strong Physics and Mathematics department. These two subjects define your ability to access engineering programs.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Aggregate 19–24 can still access Science at good Category B schools. Focus on Elective Maths and Physics performance.

careers.title: Electrical Engineer
careers.description: Design and develop electrical systems, power networks, and electronic devices. Essential for Ghana's energy sector.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Elective Mathematics — most important subject for this career
shs_guidance.note: Physics and Elective Maths are both required. Electrical Engineering is one of the most Maths-intensive careers.

wassce_requirements.special_notes: Physics and Elective Maths grades carry the most weight. Aim for B3 or better in both.

universities.name: University of Ghana
universities.notes: BSc Electrical and Electronic Engineering under CBAS.

universities.name: KNUST (College of Engineering)
universities.notes: Top engineering program in Ghana for electrical systems.

bece_guidance.advice: Mathematics and Science at JHS directly predict performance in Physics and Elective Maths at SHS. Take both seriously from JHS level.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Category B Science schools still lead here. The key differentiator is SHS performance not BECE aggregate.

careers.title: Mechanical Engineer
careers.description: Design, develop, and maintain mechanical systems and machinery. Works across manufacturing, automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Technical Drawing (strong advantage for Mechanical Engineering)
shs_guidance.note: Same core electives as other engineering programs. Technical Drawing gives a practical advantage.

wassce_requirements.special_notes: Physics and Elective Maths are the key subjects. Technical Drawing is an advantage but not universally required.

universities.name: KNUST (College of Engineering)
universities.notes: Strongest Mechanical Engineering program in Ghana.

universities.name: University of Ghana
universities.notes: Offered under the engineering sciences track.

bece_guidance.advice: Same path as other engineering careers. Physics and Maths are the foundation. Choose a Science school with practical lab facilities.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Still achievable via strong Category B Science schools with good Physics departments.

careers.title: Telecommunications Engineer
careers.description: Design and manage communication networks including mobile networks, internet infrastructure, and broadcasting systems.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Elective Mathematics — core for telecommunications systems
shs_guidance.note: Closely related to Electrical Engineering at the SHS level. Same elective pathway.

wassce_requirements.special_notes: Physics and Maths are critical. Some programs accept Computer Science elective in place of Chemistry.

university_programs.program: BSc Telecommunication Engineering / BSc Computer Engineering

universities.name: KNUST
universities.notes: Telecommunications Engineering is a distinct program at KNUST.

universities.name: University of Ghana
universities.notes: Computer Engineering at UG covers telecommunications fundamentals.

bece_guidance.advice: The same Science foundation applies. Ghana's growing telecom sector makes this an excellent career choice with strong job prospects.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Category B Science schools provide the same pathway. Focus on excelling in Physics and Maths at SHS.

careers.title: Accountant / Chartered Accountant
careers.description: Manage financial records, prepare accounts, conduct audits, and advise on financial matters. One of the most in-demand business careers in Ghana.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Elective Mathematics (strongly recommended for CA path)
shs_guidance.note: Financial Accounting at SHS is the direct foundation for university accounting programs. Cost Accounting is also useful.

wassce_requirements.special_notes: Business aggregate uses Social Studies (not Integrated Science) as the third core subject. Elective Mathematics is not required but helps with CA professional exams later.

universities.name: University of Ghana Business School (UGBS)
universities.notes: One of the top business schools in West Africa. BSc Administration with Accounting major.

universities.name: University of Cape Coast School of Business
universities.notes: Strong accounting program. Good professional exam pass rates.

bece_guidance.advice: Business programs are accessible across a wider aggregate range. Focus on Mathematics and English. Many students also pursue ICAG (Institute of Chartered Accountants Ghana) certification after university.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Category C and D schools with Business programs still lead to this career. This is one of the most accessible professional career paths.

careers.title: Banker / Finance Analyst
careers.description: Manage financial products, analyze investment opportunities, and support clients with banking and financial services.

shs_guidance.required_program: Business (preferred) or General Arts with Economics
shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Elective Mathematics
shs_guidance.note: Economics is the core subject for finance. Both Business and Arts (with Economics) students can pursue finance programs.

electives_needed: Financial Accounting or Elective Mathematics (C6 min)
wassce_requirements.special_notes: Finance programs at UG accept both Business and Arts students with Economics. Elective Maths strengthens the application.

university_programs.program: BSc Administration (Finance / Banking option) / BSc Economics

universities.name: University of Ghana Business School
universities.notes: Finance and Banking specialization under BSc Administration.

universities.name: University of Cape Coast
universities.notes: BSc Economics and BSc Finance available.

bece_guidance.advice: Strong Mathematics at JHS and SHS is the key differentiator in banking and finance careers. Elective Maths at SHS opens more doors.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Very accessible across all school categories. Business and Arts programs are available in most SHS schools.

careers.title: Marketing / Business Manager
careers.description: Plan and execute marketing strategies, manage business operations, and drive organizational growth.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Business Management and Economics are the core pair
shs_guidance.note: Both Business and Arts pathways work. Business gives the more direct route.

electives_needed: Financial Accounting or any third elective (C6 min)
wassce_requirements.special_notes: One of the more flexible entry programs. Good English and communication skills matter beyond grades.

universities.name: University of Ghana Business School
universities.notes: Marketing and Management specializations under BSc Administration.

universities.name: University of Cape Coast School of Business
universities.notes: BSc Business Administration with Management option.

bece_guidance.advice: Good communication and English skills are as important as grades in this career. Focus on English and Social Studies performance.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Very achievable path. Business programs are available at Category B, C, and D schools.

careers.title: Supply Chain / Logistics Manager
careers.description: Manage the flow of goods, services, and information from production to delivery. Growing career in Ghana's expanding trade and commerce sector.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Geography (useful for understanding trade routes and logistics)
shs_guidance.note: Business is the direct pathway. Logistics management programs are growing in Ghana.

wassce_requirements.special_notes: Similar entry requirements to general business programs.

university_programs.program: BSc Administration (Logistics & Supply Chain option) / BSc Logistics

universities.name: University of Ghana Business School
universities.notes: Supply Chain and Logistics as a specialization under BSc Administration.

universities.name: University of Cape Coast
universities.notes: Business Administration with relevant specialization.

bece_guidance.advice: This career is growing fast in Ghana due to trade expansion. Business path is straightforward and accessible.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Very accessible path. Available through most Business program SHS schools.

careers.title: Lawyer
careers.description: Represent clients in legal matters, draft legal documents, and advise on rights and obligations. One of the most prestigious careers in Ghana.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Literature in English is the most important elective for law
shs_guidance.note: English Literature and History/Government are the traditional law electives. Business students with Economics can also qualify.

wassce_requirements.special_notes: English Language grade is critically important for law. Ghana School of Law entrance exam is required after the LLB degree. KNUST Law accepts Business students with Economics electives.

universities.name: University of Ghana (Faculty of Law)
universities.notes: Most competitive law faculty in Ghana. Strong English performance essential.

universities.name: University of Cape Coast (Faculty of Law)
universities.notes: Growing law faculty. Good reputation for commercial law.

universities.name: KNUST (Faculty of Law)
universities.notes: Accepts Business students. Strong links to commercial and business law.

bece_guidance.advice: English Language is the single most important subject for a future lawyer. Read widely, write often, and focus on comprehension and composition from JHS level.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Law is still accessible via Category B Arts schools. Strong English and Literature performance at SHS matters more than BECE aggregate for this career.

careers.title: Journalist / Media Practitioner
careers.description: Report news, produce media content, and communicate information to the public through print, broadcast, or digital platforms.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Literature in English — most important for journalism
shs_guidance.note: General Arts is the natural pathway. Strong English and writing skills from early on are essential.

wassce_requirements.special_notes: English Language grade is the most important. Communication and writing ability matters beyond just grades.

universities.name: University of Ghana (School of Information and Communication Studies)
universities.notes: Ghana's leading communication and journalism school.

universities.name: University of Cape Coast
universities.notes: BA Communication Studies available.

bece_guidance.advice: Read newspapers, write regularly, and develop strong communication habits from JHS. English is everything in this career.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Very accessible path. General Arts programs are available in most SHS schools across all categories.

careers.title: Teacher / Educator
careers.description: Educate students at basic, secondary, or tertiary levels. Teaching remains one of the most important and stable careers in Ghana.

shs_guidance.required_program: Any program (Science, Arts, Business, TVET) depending on subject specialization
required_electives: Depends on subject to be taught — Science teachers need Science electives, Arts teachers need Arts electives
shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Choose electives aligned with the subject you want to teach
shs_guidance.note: Teaching is unique — your SHS program should match the subject you want to specialize in at university level.

electives_needed: 3 electives relevant to your teaching subject area
wassce_requirements.special_notes: UCC is Ghana's primary teacher education university. College of Education programs also accept students through separate placement. Aggregate requirements vary by subject area.

universities.name: University of Cape Coast (College of Education Studies)
universities.notes: Ghana's premier teacher education university. Offers B.Ed across Science, Arts, Social Studies, and Technical education.

universities.name: University of Ghana (School of Education)
universities.notes: Education programs with various subject specializations.

bece_guidance.advice: Teaching is accessible across a very wide range of aggregate scores. UCC is especially well-positioned for teacher training. Colleges of Education (CoE) are also a strong route.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Colleges of Education accept students through their own placement system and are excellent institutions for teacher training even with higher BECE aggregates.

careers.title: Automotive Technician (TVET)
careers.description: Diagnose, repair, and maintain vehicles including cars, trucks, and heavy equipment. Growing demand with Ghana's expanding vehicle fleet.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Auto Mechanics is the core elective for this career
shs_guidance.note: Technical/Vocational schools offer Auto Mechanics as a specific track. This career does not require a university degree — HND or City & Guilds certification is the standard route.

wassce_requirements.aggregate_target: Not required for HND route — TVET certificate sufficient
wassce_requirements.special_notes: TVET graduates can progress to HND at polytechnics or technical universities without the same university aggregate requirements. NVTI certification is also an alternative route.

universities.name: Accra Technical University
universities.typical_cutoff: Not aggregate-based — TVET certificate and aptitude
universities.notes: HND Automotive Engineering. Practical, industry-linked program.

universities.name: Cape Coast Technical University
universities.notes: Technical and vocational programs in engineering trades.

bece_guidance.recommended_aggregate: Any aggregate (TVET pathway is open to all)
bece_guidance.advice: Technical schools are a strong and dignified pathway. Students who enjoy hands-on work and mechanics thrive in this career. Ghana's vehicle repair industry is a major employer.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: TVET schools are specifically designed for students with a wider range of aggregates. This is a strength, not a limitation.

careers.title: Building & Construction Technician (TVET)
careers.description: Plan, supervise, and execute building and construction projects. Works alongside engineers and architects in Ghana's booming construction sector.

shs_guidance.recommended_elective: Building Construction is the core elective
shs_guidance.note: Technical schools offer Building Construction as a specific track. Can progress to HND Building Technology at technical universities.

electives_needed: Workshop Technology or Electrical Installation (C6 min)
wassce_requirements.special_notes: HND Building Technology at technical universities is the natural progression. Civil Engineering degree is an option for top performers who want the university route.

university_programs.program: HND Building Technology / HND Civil Engineering

universities.name: Accra Technical University
universities.typical_cutoff: Not aggregate-based — TVET certificate and aptitude
universities.notes: HND Building Technology. Strong construction industry links.

universities.name: Cape Coast Technical University
universities.notes: HND programs in building and civil works.

bece_guidance.recommended_aggregate: Any aggregate (TVET pathway is open to all)
bece_guidance.advice: Ghana's construction sector is one of the fastest-growing industries. Building technicians are in high demand. This career path is practical, well-paying, and respectable.
bece_guidance.if_aggregate_is_higher: Technical schools are designed for this. TVET is not a fallback — it is a focused career track with strong employment outcomes.
